title: Kanban — Architecture Tour
description: A guided walkthrough of the Kanban codebase: an agent-native task management system that orchestrates parallel AI coding agents on isolated git worktrees.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of tRPC and Zod

steps.title: Welcome to Kanban
steps.description:
Kanban is an agent-native task management platform. It runs multiple AI coding agents in parallel, each on an isolated git worktree, with a real-time web UI for monitoring and review.

The codebase splits into two main trees: a Node.js backend and a React frontend, connected via tRPC for type-safe RPC and WebSockets for live terminal streaming.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over src/ and web-ui/ to compare their sizes

steps.title: Core — API Contracts & Business Logic
steps.description:
This module is the heart of the backend. It defines every API contract with Zod schemas, validates incoming payloads, and implements task board mutations — creation, state transitions, and linking.

The agent catalog discovers available coding agents such as Cline, Claude, Codex, and Gemini.

interactiveActions.description: Click api-contract.ts to see the Zod schema definitions

steps.title: tRPC — Type-Safe API Layer
steps.description:
This module exposes the backend to the frontend via type-safe RPC endpoints. The app router composes sub-routers for task execution, workspace operations, lifecycle hooks, and project selection.

Types flow end-to-end: Zod schemas in core, to tRPC routers, to auto-inferred client types in the frontend.

interactiveActions.description: Click app-router.ts to see how routers compose

steps.title: Server — Runtime State & HTTP
steps.description:
The runtime backbone. A central state store tracks all active tasks and broadcasts updates via SSE. The workspace registry manages workspace lifecycles, and the HTTP server ties it all together.

Everything the frontend queries flows through here.

interactiveActions.description: Click runtime-state-hub.ts — it's the central nervous system

steps.title: Workspace — Git Worktrees & File Management
steps.description:
This module makes parallel agent execution possible. Each task gets its own ephemeral git worktree so agents can work independently without conflicts.

The highlighted files cover worktree lifecycle, change syncing, diff detection, commit history, and progress snapshots for rollback.

interactiveActions.description: Click task-worktree.ts to see worktree lifecycle management

steps.title: Terminal — Agent Session Orchestration
steps.description:
This module bridges agent execution with the UI. It orchestrates session lifecycles and adapts different agent SDKs into a common interface.

A registry discovers installed agents, a WebSocket server streams live output to the frontend, and a pseudo-terminal wrapper enables interactive shell sessions.

interactiveActions.description: Click session-manager.ts — the agent session orchestrator

steps.title: Cline SDK — Native Agent Integration
steps.description:
This module provides deep integration with the Cline agent framework. Rather than running Cline as a subprocess, Kanban embeds it natively.

It manages per-task Cline sessions, sets up the runtime environment, converts Cline events into Kanban's event model, configures LLM providers, and integrates the Model Context Protocol.

interactiveActions.description: Click cline-task-session-service.ts to see how Cline is embedded

steps.title: Frontend — React Components & Board UI
steps.description:
This directory holds the React UI — roughly 70 files. The board core handles the main layout with drag-and-drop columns, individual task cards showing live agent status, and an expanded detail view with terminal, chat, and diff panels.

Sub-directories group detail panels for terminal, chat, and diff viewers, a commit history browser, and the design system primitives shared across the app.

interactiveActions.description: Click kanban-board.tsx to see the board layout

steps.title: Frontend — Custom Hooks & Logic Layer
steps.description:
Business logic is extracted from components into over 50 custom hooks. This separation keeps components focused on rendering while hooks handle board state and mutations, agent session control, git operations, keyboard shortcuts, and terminal I/O.

The runtime directory connects hooks to the backend via the tRPC client and SSE subscriptions.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over hooks/ to see how much logic lives outside components

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description:
You've seen every major layer — the React frontend delegates to tRPC, which invokes Zod-validated core logic, which fans out to workspace and terminal infrastructure. Real-time updates flow back via WebSocket and SSE.

This is the same full-map view you started with. Explore freely.

interactiveActions.description: Explore the codebase freely — you now know where everything lives
